question.stem: What does the passage suggest about effective vocabulary review?

options.A: Students should memorize every word before reading.
options.B: Words are easier to remember when reviewed with context.
options.C: Exam questions never require vocabulary knowledge.
options.D: Long word lists are always more useful than short notes.

evidence.text: students who review words in context often remember them longer

high_yield_items.why_it_matters: Connects the option to the passage evidence.
Explains why context-based review is effective.

paraphrases.note: Same review method expressed with a different grammatical form.

logic_signals.note: Signals that the passage contrasts long word lists with context-based review.

distractor_traps.why_it_is_tempting: It repeats the phrase long word lists.
distractor_traps.why_it_fails: The passage contrasts long lists with a more useful context-based method.

low_vocab_path: Find the repeated topic word: review.
Match context in the option with context in the passage.
Use However to avoid choosing the surface-overlap distractor.
